calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-15-2026 Granted Per Written Order
TENTATIVE RULING: Granted per written order.
The Court waives defective notice for this hearing only. Notice did not have the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice; a printout of pages of the local rules that do not even address remote appearance or provide a link to where the remote instructions can be found online or were not part of the Probate division's local rules is not sufficient. Any future notices must have the printed out instructions attached to the notice. Both the mailed and published notice indicated that this was a hearing of the petition AND the amended petition. An amended pleading completely restates the supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3.] Notice was mailed to Ashley Johnson at an address that differs from the one reported to be hers in the amendment filed on 3/16/26. Issues raised by the Court should just be addressed by filing the missing papers (or withdrawing an erroneous filing); there is no need to declare that they will be done. The child of a first cousin is a first cousin once removed. The grandchild of a first cousin is a first cousin twice removed. The actual ages of adults do not need to be set forth in item 8 of a Petition for Probate; "Adult" is sufficient. (Ages of minors must be set forth.)
Status of closing the estate will be set for 7/26/27.
The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of a report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 7/26/27. Appearance will be required on 7/26/27 if no petition has been filed.

EXAMINER: HELEN TROWBRIDGE
